_comment: Golden inputs for the LIVE scoring eval (app/evals/live_scoring.py). Each case is a hand-authored SYNTHETIC applicant + one dimension, run through the REAL production scoring prompt+model. Fictional data only (no real applicants), so it is safe to commit. `expect` states the crisp, deterministic property the output must satisfy; `judge` (optional) asks the rubric judge the subjective question. Grow by adding cases; keep applicants terse.

cases.note: Applicant never mentions trade skills -> silence must score 0 (neutral), never negative.

essays.introduce_yourself: We love reading and community potlucks.
essays.why_coop: We want neighbours who look out for each other.

dimension.name: Licensed trade skills
dimension.definition: Whether an adult holds a licensed/certified trade (electrician, plumber, gasfitter, Red Seal) they explicitly offer toward co-op maintenance.
dimension.high_end: At least one adult holds a current trade licence and explicitly offers it for co-op maintenance.
dimension.low_end: No adult holds or mentions any licensed trade.

essays.skills: I'm a Red Seal electrician and I'll handle the co-op's electrical maintenance and safety checks myself.
essays.why_coop: I want to contribute my trade to a place I live.

dimension.name: Licensed trade skills
dimension.definition: Whether an adult holds a licensed/certified trade (electrician, plumber, gasfitter, Red Seal) they explicitly offer toward co-op maintenance.
dimension.high_end: At least one adult holds a current trade licence and explicitly offers it for co-op maintenance.
dimension.low_end: No adult holds or mentions any licensed trade.

cases.judge: Does the cited evidence genuinely justify a high (near +1) score on this dimension?

cases.note: Applicant addresses the dimension and states a genuine limitation -> a real negative, not silence.

essays.available_time: I'm honest that between touring most weekends and two other boards I sit on, my calendar is packed — I could only manage the bare minimum co-op hours.

dimension.name: Realistic available time for co-op work
dimension.definition: How much discretionary time the household realistically has for co-op shifts and committee work after accounting for self-reported external commitments.
dimension.high_end: Sparse outside commitments and abundant free time; offers more than the minimum share.
dimension.low_end: A genuinely packed schedule that leaves only minimum hours for co-op duties.

cases.judge: Does the cited evidence genuinely justify a low (negative) score on this dimension?

cases.note: The regression that motivated the signed scale: low_end is WORDED as absence, applicant is silent -> must still be 0, not negative.

essays.introduce_yourself: We're quiet, reliable, and keep to ourselves mostly.

dimension.name: Governance / board experience
dimension.definition: Prior experience serving on a board, council, or committee that involved shared decision-making.
dimension.high_end: Has served on a board/council/committee and describes the governance work.
dimension.low_end: No board, council, or committee experience mentioned anywhere.
